summary.note: Raw pass rate drop is misleading — see analyst observations below. ~8 failures are eval-design issues (testing validation-step techniques when eval stops at step 03a, brief-type-conditional assertions). Adjusted pass rate excluding structurally unfair assertions: ~96%

evals.name: consumer-hot-sauce
failures: Ownability-first compounds in parallel (FALSE NEGATIVE: skill correctly skips for consumer food briefs)
Gallery mode with H/W/C (format unclear — scannable table used but without H/W/C abbreviation)

evals.name: consumer-streetwear
failures: Desired-Self Naming (metadata claim but no visible output)
Trend-Cycle Audit (completely absent)
Memetic/Viral Engineering (completely absent)

evals.name: enterprise-cybersecurity
failures: Analyst/Press Headline Testing (Gartner tested but Forbes not)

evals.name: enterprise-data-integration
failures: Sales Conversation Fit templates not shown (integration grammar tested instead)
Prefix x Suffix Matrix not used (ad-hoc combinations instead)
Morphological Analysis (Zwicky) explicitly in techniques_not_yet_used
Riff volume: 3 of 6 directions below 10 (8-9 each)
Internal-Champion Forwardability not explicitly tested

evals.name: enterprise-legaltech
failures: Buyer Committee test (STRUCTURAL: belongs in Steps 04a-04f, not reached)
Sales Conversation Fit (STRUCTURAL: belongs in Steps 04a-04f, not reached)
Enterprise Objection Preemption (STRUCTURAL: belongs in Steps 04a-04f, not reached)
Cross-language checks (STRUCTURAL: systematic screening belongs in Step 04, basic notes present)
Three-Team Method (explicitly in techniques_not_yet_used)
Riff technique labels missing (riffs describe meaning, not technique)
Riff volume inflated by duplicates (actual new unique riffs: 4-7 per direction)
Homonym/Synonym/Sound-Alike Riffing (not demonstrably applied)
Conflict flagging without warning emoji format
Category Creation Naming not considered

iteration_1_failures_fixed: Hot sauce: Shelf Test now formally tracked (was informal in iter-1)
Hot sauce: Social-native testing now formally tracked (was informal in iter-1)
Legaltech: SCAMPER now applied per B2B riffing checklist (was skipped in iter-1)

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Eval design flaw: assertions test techniques from Steps 04a-04f but eval stops at Step 03a
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Legaltech has 5-6 failures for Buyer Committee, Sales Conversation Fit, Enterprise Objection Preemption, Cross-language checks, Category Creation, and Lifecycle Stress Test — all of which belong in validation steps that were never reached. These are structural false negatives. The data integration eval also fails Sales Conversation Fit for the same reason.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Either run evals through the full pipeline OR tag assertions with which step produces them and grade only reachable assertions

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Eval design flaw: ownability-first compound assertion not conditional on brief type
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Hot sauce correctly skips the ownability-first compound track per SKILL.md consumer/food guidance, but the assertion expects it regardless. This is a false negative.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Make assertion conditional: 'Ownability-first compounds generated in parallel (B2B briefs) OR compound track correctly skipped (consumer/fashion/food briefs)'

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Genuine skill gap: consumer techniques Desired-Self, Trend-Cycle, Memetic/Viral not reliably triggered
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Streetwear eval failed all 3. Desired-Self appears in metadata but has no visible output. Trend-Cycle and Memetic/Viral are completely absent. These are real gaps in the consumer-naming pathway.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Add explicit prompts for these techniques in step-02a or step-02c for consumer briefs

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Genuine skill gap: Prefix x Suffix Matrix and Morphological Analysis (Zwicky) not reliably triggered
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Data integration eval explicitly has Morphological Analysis in techniques_not_yet_used. Prefix x Suffix Matrix was not used despite being in the assertions. These advanced generation techniques are available but not being invoked.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Consider making these mandatory for enterprise briefs in step-02a, or remove from assertions if they're intended as optional

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Genuine skill gap: riff technique labeling inconsistent across evals
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Hot sauce, streetwear, skincare, cybersecurity, and data integration all properly label riff techniques. Legaltech does NOT — riffs describe meaning instead of technique. This suggests the instruction is followed ~83% of the time.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Riffing step (02c) could reinforce the labeling requirement more explicitly

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Genuine skill gap: riff volume per direction sometimes falls short of 10
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Data integration: 3 of 6 directions at 8-9 (below 10). Legaltech: actual unique riffs per direction are 4-7 (counts inflated by duplicates). Other evals meet the threshold.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Consider lowering the threshold to 8 per direction, or allow total riff count (e.g., 60+) as an alternative metric

cross_cutting_observations.detail: Streetwear uses ⚠️ emoji correctly. Hot sauce uses parenthetical text. Legaltech uses plain text. Data integration uses ⚠️. The skill specifies emoji but enforcement is inconsistent.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Minor formatting issue — the substance (conflict identification) is consistently present even when emoji format varies

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Baselines are surprisingly competent at raw name volume
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Without skill: avg 111 names in 142s. With skill: avg 184 names in 732s. The skill adds 66% more names but the real value is methodology (technique diversity, structural tracking, domain-specific testing, artifacts with YAML frontmatter, hard gates, consumer/enterprise pathway selection). Name count alone understates the skill's contribution.
cross_cutting_observations.recommendation: Consider adding qualitative assertions about methodology depth, not just name count

cross_cutting_observations.detail: Hot sauce Shelf Test: PASS. Hot sauce Social-native: PASS. Legaltech SCAMPER: PASS (on B2B checklist, confirmed complete). The skill changes made after iteration-1 worked.
